methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Skyeton operates in the Defense & Security sector at the Growth stage. The comparable universe encompasses 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. This peer set provides sector context but lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier clustering for robust multiples-based analysis.

Peer-Median valuation methodology is not applicable to Skyeton. The Defense & Security sector contains 21 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for stage alignment and valuation tier yields fewer than three comparable datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable multiples derivation. Applying all-sector medians would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and technology maturity across Defense & Security subsegments.

Given the data constraints, this analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation range with quantitative rigor. A credible assessment would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent sectors with comparable growth profiles and capital intensity, (2) access to specific financial metrics (revenue, growth rates, EBITDA multiples) from the identified private peers, or (3) application of venture-stage DCF methods with explicit assumptions around addressable market and unit economics. The current dataset confirms Skyeton's sector classification and peer universe but does not enable the cross-sectional analysis necessary for a standalone valuation conclusion.
